rulings.Case Title: JUDY L DEVORE et al VS. ABB AUTOMATION INC., INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Parsons Infrastructure & Technology Group Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff has failed to raise a triable issue whether the systems in question contained asbestos. Plaintiff's discovery responses and deposition testimony were devoid of specific information showing that defendant's employees disturbed asbestos-containing packing or gaskets associated with control valves and/or level transmitters. Plaintiff failed to present sufficient evidence that the system designed, procured and built by Defendant was asbestos-containing or specified asbestos-containing component parts. Kenneth Cohen's declaration does not create a triable issue because that declaration lacks a sufficient foundation for his conclusion regarding asbestos content. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCIS FANELLI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant A.O. Smith Corporation's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production on absence of asbestos content based on plaintiffs' factually devoid discovery responses. Plaintiffs failed to create a triable issue regarding asbestos content. Charles Ay's conclusion regarding asbestos content lacks a sufficient foundation. Ay is experienced in industrial and commercial construction, not home heating systems. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCIS FANELLI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Sloan Valve Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Sloan's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to causation (issues 1-2 and 4). Sloan's alternate motion for summary adjudication is moot as to false representation and punitive damages (issues 3 and 5), as plaintiffs dismissed these claims against Sloan on 1/12/2010. Sloan did not satisfy its burden of production on causation. The declaration Peter Jahrling does not negate the possibility that plaintiff was exposed to a Sloan asbestos-containing valve and plaintiffs' responses to contention interrogatories were not factually devoid. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEONARD SHIELDS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Orchard Supply Hardware LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied. Declaration of Leonard Shields creates triable issue of fact of exposure to asbestos containing tile purchased from Orchard. Orchard's objections to the declaration are overruled. Summary adjudication as to cause of action for false representation granted. Orchard has shifted the burden of production with factually devoid discovery responses and Shields produces no evidence of any representation made by Orchard to Shields =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEONARD SHIELDS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 22, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Utility Trailer Manufacturing's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of production with respect to the issues of exposure to original brakes on a Utility Trailer, the asbestos content of those brakes, causation, and whether Mr. Shields suffers from an asbestos-related disease. Defendant did not propound "all facts" interrogatories and Mr. Shields testified at deposition that he removed original brakes from Utility Trailers. He was not asked if those brakes contained asbestos. Mr. Shields provides a sufficient foundation for his statement that the brakes were original. Defendant's objections to his testimony are overruled. Mr. Stanton's declaration that the brakes "may or may not have contained asbestos" is insufficient to negate an element of plaintiffs' claims. Mr. Shields' deposition alone does not show that he cannot reasonably obtain information that he suffers from an asbestos-related disease. The deposition testimony of Dr. Douglas Freeman does not negate this element because he does not know if there is a correlation between nonsmall cell carcinoma and asbestos exposure and does not know if Mr Shields' atelectasis was caused by asbestos exposure. =(220/HEK)
